name: Systemic CASIN extended lifespan in aged female mice
summary: A later mouse study connects systemic Cdc42 inhibition to lifespan and inflammatory/epigenetic age markers, but remains preclinical.
statement: A four-day systemic CASIN course in 75-week-old female C57BL/6 mice reportedly extended average and maximum lifespan while lowering aging-associated inflammatory cytokines and blood DNA-methylation age.
time_horizon: Post-treatment lifespan follow-up after a four-day intervention
quantitative_note: The study reports significant extension of average and maximum lifespan; the staged baseline treats it as animal lifespan evidence, not human-relevant proof.
caveats: Single-species, female-mouse evidence is not enough for human forecast confidence.
The mechanism may involve systemic inflammation and blood-cell epigenetic age, not only stem-cell-intrinsic rejuvenation.
Translation depends on whether systemic Cdc42 inhibition can be made safe and selective.
